title: Find Peak Element
description:
Given an array nums of integers, find a peak element and return its index. An element is a peak if it is greater than or equal to its neighbors. For corner elements, we need to consider only one neighbor.

Your solution should have a complexity of O(log n).

examples.explanation: In the array [1, 2, 3, 1], the element 3 at index 2 is greater than its neighbors (2 and 1), thus it is a peak.
In the array [1, 2, 1, 3, 5, 6, 4], both 2 at index 1 and 6 at index 5 are peaks as they are greater than their neighbors.
For the single element array [1], the only element 1 is considered a peak since there are no neighbors.

constraints: The input array nums must have at least one element.
Each element in nums can be any integer.
hints: Consider using binary search to find the peak efficiently.
Check the mid-point and compare it with its neighbors to decide the direction to move.
Remember that the first or last element can also be a peak.
Think about the properties of the problem when high and low indices converge.
